title: alnus lusitanica
intro: Alnus lusitanica, also known as Portuguese alder, is a deciduous tree species native to the Iberian Peninsula. The bark, leaves, and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to various medicinal benefits. The health benefits of Alnus lusitanica include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
